deck_title: Robust and Reproducible Evaluation of Error-Rate Control in High-Dimensional Discrete and Heterogeneous Data

slides.title: Presentation Overview and Research Scope
key_points: Introduce the intersection of high-dimensional statistics and discrete data analysis in modern bioinformatics。
State the objective to establish reproducible evaluation frameworks for error-rate control。
Outline the presentation structure covering methodology, comparison, and application domains。

slides.title: Multiple Testing Challenges in High-Dimensional Settings
key_points: Explain the proliferation of hypothesis testing in genomic and omics data science。
Identify limitations of classical multiple testing methods when applied to discrete and heterogeneous data。
Articulate the reproducibility crisis in statistical methodology and its impact on scientific conclusions。

slides.title: Characteristics of Discrete and Heterogeneous Data
key_points: Describe zero-inflated and over-dispersed distributions common in RNA-seq and NGS data。
Explain heterogeneity across experimental conditions, platforms, and batch effects。
Note computational constraints in high-dimensional settings with discrete features。

slides.title: The Multiple Testing Problem in Genomics
key_points: Discuss Bonferroni correction limitations in controlling family-wise error rates for large-scale testing。
Explain why false discovery rate control is preferred for high-dimensional genomic applications。
Identify specific challenges with discrete count data and integer-valued distributions。

slides.title: Proposed Methodological Framework
key_points: Outline the hierarchical approach to error control integrating discrete-aware and heterogeneous procedures。
Describe the simulation framework for validation and reproducibility。
Specify the R-based implementation strategy for reproducible research。

slides.title: False Discovery Rate Control Fundamentals
key_points: Define FDR as the expected proportion of false positives among rejected hypotheses。
Explain the Benjamini-Hochberg procedure's theoretical properties under independence assumptions。
Discuss assumptions and limitations underlying standard FDR control methods。

slides.title: False Discovery Exceedance Analysis
key_points: Introduce FDX as a measure of worst-case error rates for risk-averse applications。
Explain how FDX complements FDR in comprehensive error assessment frameworks。
Describe the relationship between FDX and permutation-based false discovery control。

slides.title: Discrete-Aware Testing Procedures
key_points: Explain why integer-valued data requires specialized testing methods beyond continuous approximations。
Describe permutation-based approaches for discrete data maintaining exact FDR control。
Outline the challenges in maintaining FDR control with discrete distributions and small sample sizes。

slides.title: Handling Heterogeneous Experimental Designs
key_points: Address batch effects and platform differences in high-dimensional data analysis。
Explain hierarchical modeling approaches for heterogeneous data across conditions。
Describe adaptive procedures for varying data quality and experimental designs。

slides.title: Comparing Benjamini-Hochberg and Benjamini-Yekutieli
key_points: Contrast the independence assumptions of BH with BY's handling of arbitrary dependence。
Evaluate computational efficiency differences between BH and BY procedures。
Discuss practical implications for genomic applications with correlated features。

slides.title: Discrete-Aware BH Variants: SU and SD
key_points: Explain the Superiority (SU) and Superiority-Down (SD) modifications for discrete data。
Describe how these methods improve FDR control under discrete distributions。
Compare their performance characteristics with standard BH and BY procedures。

slides.title: Hierarchical and Group-Based Procedures
key_points: Describe hierarchical testing for structured hypotheses in genomic pathways。
Explain group-based procedures for correlated features and gene sets。
Outline the integration with FDR control in hierarchical testing frameworks。

slides.title: Simulation Methodology and Reproducible R Implementation
key_points: Detail the simulation design for comparative evaluation of error-rate control methods。
Describe the R package architecture for reproducible research and code sharing。
Specify the metrics for performance assessment including FDR, FDX, power, and FDP stability。

slides.title: Applications and Scientific Contributions
key_points: Highlight applications in GWAS, RNA-seq, and NGS read-count data analysis。
Summarize expected contributions to statistical methodology and bioinformatics。
Outline future directions for discrete data analysis and reproducible research。
